title: Dryopteris borreri
intro: Dryopteris borreri, also known as Borrer's male fern or wood fern, is a species of fern native to the British Isles and Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including rheumatism, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris borreri include flavonoids, lignans, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often made into infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being used in poultices and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
